title: Define and Call a Function
instruction: Define a function `greet(name string) string` that returns `"Hello, " + name + "!"`. Call it with "Alice" and print the result.
hint: func greet(name string) string { return "Hello, " + name + "!" }

title: Multiple Return Values
instruction: Define `divide(a, b float64) (float64, error)` that returns an error if b is zero, otherwise returns a/b. Call with 10 and 2, then print the result.
hint: if b == 0 { return 0, errors.New("division by zero") }; return a / b, nil

title: Variadic Functions
instruction: Define `sum(nums ...int) int` that returns the total of all arguments. Call it with 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 and print the result.
hint: func sum(nums ...int) int { total := 0; for _, n := range nums { total += n }; return total }

title: Named Return Values
instruction: Define `minMax(nums []int) (min, max int)` that returns the smallest and largest value. Call with [3, 1, 4, 1, 5] and print both.
hint: func minMax(nums []int) (min, max int) { min, max = nums[0], nums[0]; for _, n := range nums { if n < min { min = n }; if n > max { max = n } }; return }

title: Functions as Values
instruction: Define `apply(f func(int) int, x int) int` that returns `f(x)`. Call it with a doubling function and 2, then print the result.
hint: func apply(f func(int) int, x int) int { return f(x) }

title: Closures
instruction: Define `counter()` that returns a function which increments and returns an internal count each time it's called. Call the returned function three times and print each result (1, 2, 3).
hint: func counter() func() int { count := 0; return func() int { count++; return count } }
